title: Qld falls into line with six-member funds
full_text:

summary: Queensland has decided to align its superannuation funds with a six-member structure, which is expected to improve governance and efficiency. This change aims to enhance the management of retirement savings for Queenslanders.
heading: Superannuation and Investments
key_points: Queensland adopts a six-member fund structure.
The move is aimed at better governance.
Expected to improve efficiency in managing superannuation.
